title: Report of the EST and of the 2025 Annual Membership Meeting

article.introduction:
The Eighty-Fifth Annual Membership Meeting was held at the Marriott Marquis in San Francisco, California, on January 4, 2025, at 5:30 pm PST. The video of the meeting has been posted on the AFA website. Executive Secretary and Treasurer Kathleen Hanley called the meeting to order and welcomed those in attendance.
EST Hanley reported on the financial position of the AFA. For the past fiscal year, operating income (unaudited), without changes in investments and Fischer Black Prize fund contributions, was $1,576,383 and total expenses were $2,273,534 for a net income of –$697,151. Total assets as of June 30, 2024, are currently $26.8 million for the Association's portfolio and $1.5 million for the Fischer-Black Prize endowment.
The Investment Committee, composed of Stijn Van Nieuwerburgh, Wei Xiong, and Wei Jiang proposed investment guidelines that were approved by the board. A copy of the policy can be found on the AFA website.
Membership totals for the past 10 years are presented in the following graph (2024 numbers are preliminary).

The Annual Meeting for 2026 is scheduled to be held January 3–5 in Philadelphia (Saturday, Sunday, Monday). For the following three years, the ASSA Meetings are scheduled as follows:
2027 January 3–5 Washington, DC (Sunday, Monday, Tuesday)
2028 January 7–9 Atlanta, GA (Friday, Saturday, Sunday)
2029 January 5–7 San Diego (Friday, Saturday, Sunday)
President Monika Piazzesi announced the results of the 2024 election as follows:
President: Ulrike Malmendier
President Elect: Wei Jiang
Vice President: Sydney Ludvigson
Directors: Reena Aggarwal, Nicolae Gârleanu, Kelly Shue
President Piazzesi thanked the candidates who ran for election as well as the Nominating Committee and the outgoing Directors: Camelia Kuhnen, Michelle Lowry, Laura Starks, and Stijn Van Nieuwerburgh.
President Piazzesi recognized the AFA committees for their work in 2024. She reminded the membership to contact the AFA Ombuds for help with ethics concerns and directed them to the AFA website for more information.
President Elect Ulrike Malmendier, the 2025 Program Chair, reported on the Annual Meeting. Submissions are up by 10% with a corresponding decrease in the acceptance rate from 13% to 11%. In addition, while registrations have gone down since the pandemic, participation in the sessions remains robust. She encouraged the membership to submit ideas for keeping the meetings engaging. She recognized the hard work of the session chairs in organizing the 65 regular paper sessions, five panel sessions, AFA Lecture, and AFA PhD Student Poster Session. She encouraged members who missed the AFA panels or AFA Lecture to view the recordings on the AFA website. She noted an increase in the number of submissions related to AI and machine learning for the 2025 program. Finally, she mentioned that the AFA will continue to foster mentoring and will be expanding on the work of AFFECT to enlarge the mentoring program in 2025.
The AFA Lecture was delivered by Nicholas Barberis, Yale University. The title of his lecture was “Behavioral Finance at 40: Progress, Open Questions, and New Directions.” Recordings of the Panel Sessions and AFA Lecture can be found on the AFA website.
Vice President Wei Jiang reported on the AFA PhD Student Panel and AFA PhD Student Poster Session for 2025. There was a 20% increase in submissions, and the acceptance rate of 23% was maintained from last year which led to a total of 127 posters on the AFA PhD Student Poster Session. A team of 10 assistant professors helped with the selection of the posters. This year a Coffee Break was held during the afternoon to encourage attendance at the Poster Session. Vice President Jiang congratulated the students participating in the Poster Session and encouraged AFA members to view the posters and provide feedback to the students.
The Journal of FinanceEditor Antoinette Schoar informed the membership on the state of theJF(see her report in this issue for details). She also presented the 2024 Brattle Group and Dimensional Fund Advisors Prizes. The Association thanks the Brattle Group and Dimensional Fund Advisors for their generous support of financial research. She also announced that the editorial team has been renewed for a second term with Adi Sunderam (Harvard) replacing Urban Jermann (University of Pennsylvania).
Past President Markus Brunnermeier announced thatTheJournal of Finance:Insights and Perspectives(JF:I&P) will begin taking submissions within the next several months and that an editorial team has been approved by the AFA Board of Directors. The Executive Editor for theJF:I&Pis Janice Eberly (Northwestern University) with Coeditors Valentin Haddad (University of California – Los Angeles), Arvind Krishnamurthy (Stanford University), and David Thesmar (Massachusetts Institute of Technology).
Past President Brunnermeier then announced the winner of the Fischer Black Prize for 2025: Tyler Muir from the University California, Los Angeles. Professor Muir accepted the award acknowledging valuable collaborations with colleagues, coauthors, discussants, and referees. He thanked several individuals and his family for their advice and support.
Finally, Past President Brunnermeier explained the process for the selection of the annual AFA Fellow. By vote of the current AFA Fellows, Steven Kaplan has been selected as the 2025 Fellow of the Association. Professor Kaplan could not be present at the meetings and provided recorded remarks, thanking several individuals and the Association for the award.
There being no further business, the AFA business meeting was closed, and President-Elect Ulrike Malmendier introduced Monika Piazzesi for the Presidential Address.
Respectfully submitted,
Kathleen Hanley
Report of the Executive Secretary and Treasurer
For the Fiscal Year Ending June 30, 2024
